hypothesis: The computational cost of HMC refinement in AMDEN scales prohibitively with system size, but because the noise energy landscape ˆEθ learned by the energy-based score function implicitly encodes the rugged, funnel-free potential energy landscape characteristic of amorphous materials, a surrogate-accelerated variant that trains a lightweight auxiliary graph neural network to predict HMC acceptance probabilities from pre-HMC structural descriptors will reduce the number of full model evaluations required per denoising step by at least 50% without statistically significant degradation in the structural fidelity or property accuracy of generated multi-element glass structures.
